tagline: Now generally available for structured data extraction.
what_it_does: The `ai_extract` function enables the extraction of structured data from unstructured text, documents, and images based on a user-defined schema. It supports complex data types including nested objects and arrays, and provides features like type validation, citation tracking, and confidence scoring. This function is integrated with a visual UI for building and refining extraction agents, allowing users to optimize their data extraction processes.
why_it_matters: This update enhances data accessibility and usability for organizations by transforming unstructured data into actionable insights, improving reporting and analysis capabilities. It benefits data analysts and business leaders by streamlining workflows, reducing manual data processing time, and enabling more informed decision-making based on structured data extraction.
plain_summary: The ai extract function enables the extraction of structured data from unstructured text, documents, and images based on a user defined schema. It supports complex data types including nested objects and arrays, and provides features like type validation…

capabilities.title: Schema-Based Extraction
capabilities.desc: Extracts data according to a user-defined schema, allowing for tailored data insights.

capabilities.title: Visual UI for Agents
capabilities.desc: Provides a no-code interface to create and manage extraction agents easily.

capabilities.title: Support for Nested Structures
capabilities.desc: Handles complex data types including nested objects and arrays for comprehensive data extraction.

capabilities.title: Confidence Scoring
capabilities.desc: Offers confidence scores for extracted data, enhancing the reliability of insights.

prerequisites: Access to a serverless usage policy with a nonzero budget.
Unstructured data must be in a Unity Catalog volume or table.
use_cases: Extracting key information from medical records.
limitations: Maximum context length for agents is 128k tokens.
Union schema types are not supported.

steps.title: Create Agent
steps.desc: Navigate to Agents and select Create Agent > Information Extraction.

steps.title: Select Data
steps.desc: Choose files or tables from which to extract information.

steps.title: Define Schema
steps.desc: Configure the extraction schema using natural language or manual entry.

steps.title: Run Extraction
steps.desc: Save and run the extraction to see results based on the defined schema.

steps.title: Use Agent
steps.desc: Deploy the agent for SQL queries or create a Spark Declarative Pipeline.

key_takeaway: The `ai_extract` function is now generally available, enabling efficient extraction of structured data from unstructured sources.
